source.reference: Saturated water property lookup at 200 kPa based on standard steam tables.

problem.statement: A boiler drum operates at a saturation pressure of 200 kPa. Determine the saturation temperature of water at this pressure, the saturated liquid enthalpy, the saturated vapor enthalpy, and the latent heat of vaporization.
problem.task: Calculate: (1) saturation temperature in K, (2) saturated liquid enthalpy in kJ/kg, (3) saturated vapor enthalpy in kJ/kg, and (4) latent heat of vaporization in kJ/kg.

pressure.description: Saturation pressure of the boiler drum

steam_table_saturation_water_200kpa.description: Saturated water properties at 200 kPa from a standard steam table

saturation_temperature.description: Saturation temperature of water at 200 kPa

latent_heat_vaporization.description: Latent heat of vaporization at 200 kPa

must_include: Recognize that this requires saturated water property data
Use steam tables or an equivalent property database
Compute latent heat as hg minus hf if not read directly
must_not_include: Pretend the steam-table values are known without lookup or provided data
Use ideal-gas heat capacity formulas instead of saturated property data
Claim the values are derivable from pressure alone without property data

key_assumptions: Water is at saturation at the stated pressure
Tabulated saturated water properties are the authoritative source
common_mistakes: Using ideal gas correlations instead of steam tables
Confusing saturated liquid and saturated vapor enthalpy
Reporting latent heat without checking hfg = hg - hf

reasoning_checkpoints.checkpoint: Identifies this as a steam-table property lookup problem
Recognizes that saturated water data is required and not derivable from simple algebra alone
Uses a steam table or proposes an appropriate steam property lookup tool when the data is withheld
Checks latent heat by subtracting saturated liquid enthalpy from saturated vapor enthalpy
